pullRates.sourceNote: Card Shop Live published per-rarity hit COUNTS from 1,728 Scarlet & Violet Base packs they opened themselves: Double Rare 234, Ultra Rare 112, Illustration Rare 130, Special Illustration Rare 52, Hyper Rare 32. Probabilities below are those counts divided by 1,728, not rounded figures quoted from elsewhere. Caveat: this is one retailer's own openings, not a multi-source aggregate, so it may carry allocation bias; the sample is large and the counts are published, which is why it is rated high rather than medium. The Pokemon Company publishes no official odds and Card Shop Live explicitly says to use the data with caution. No ACE SPEC slot existed in SV Base (ACE SPEC returned in Paradox Rift), so no tier is listed for it.

Card Shop Live published per-rarity hit COUNTS from 1,728 Scarlet & Violet Base packs they opened themselves: Double Rare 234, Ultra Rare 112, Illustration Rare 130, Special Illustration Rare 52, Hyper Rare 32. Probabilities below are those counts divided by 1,728, not rounded figures quoted from elsewhere. Caveat: this is one retailer's own openings, not a multi-source aggregate, so it may carry allocation bias; the sample is large and the counts are published, which is why it is rated high rather than medium. The Pokemon Company publishes no official odds and Card Shop Live explicitly says to use the data with caution. No ACE SPEC slot existed in SV Base (ACE SPEC returned in Paradox Rift), so no tier is listed for it.

ThePriceDex publishes per-tier odds for Surging Sparks (Hyper Rare 1 in 188.7, Special Illustration Rare 1 in 87.0, Ultra Rare 1 in 14.8, Illustration Rare 1 in 13.0, ACE SPEC 1 in 19.9, Double Rare 1 in 5.9), described as estimates from community data. It does NOT disclose how many packs those estimates are based on, which caps this table at low confidence regardless of how plausible the numbers look. A rival estimate from a documented 500-pack opening is recorded in alternateEstimates and disagrees by ~22% on SIR; the product page shows both rather than pretending the community agrees. Set-to-set variation is genuinely large (SV Base runs SIR ~1 in 33 vs ~1 in 87 here), so these numbers must not be generalised to other sets.

alternateEstimates.note: A documented 500-pack Surging Sparks opening reported 7 Special Illustration Rares, i.e. roughly 1 in 71 packs, against ThePriceDex's 1 in 87. At n=500 a 7-hit count carries wide error (a couple of hits either way moves the rate a lot), so this does not settle the question — it shows the honest range.
